entry.title: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
entry.content:
Accelerating innovation while future-proofing your business

(Note: This is a fast-moving project so please bookmark this blog as we will keep updating the information as it becomes available)

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) is the gold standard for enterprise and global payroll with localization in 57 countries (52 by SAP, 5 by partners) and live productive usage in 84 (and counting). With more than 2000 companies around the globe, it is delivering more than 200 million pay statements per year.

ECP is SAP’s go-to, long-term payroll solution fueled by significant ongoing investment. Over last few months, we have embarked on a journey to revamp your favorite payroll product.  We are converting ECP to a modern technology foundation which will unlock new capabilities and elevate its performance for decades to come. We are working hard so these innovations reach our global customers quickly

Why is SAP embarking on this initiative?

The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027. This has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the new technology foundation as well as new tools to improve on our service delivery while enabling you to future-proof your business for years to come.

What are the benefits to our customers?

Among the key capabilities and innovations our customers will be able to experience through this technology modernization are:

  • Rollout of new features to meet customer demand
    • Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for regulated industries (e.g. public sector, healthcare, higher education)
    • Business AI capabilities to drive HR operations efficiency (e.g. Explain my pay statement)
    • Updated HORIZON UI for a unified user experience (consistent with the rest of SAP SuccessFactors HCM)
    • Enhanced Payroll Control Center capabilities to better manage payroll activities

  • Operational  enhancements
    • High availability set-up for production systems
    • Ability to get enhanced encryption (planned)

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

  1. Why is SAP doing this?
    • The current technologies used for ECP are going out of maintenance by end of 2027 which has opened an opportunity for us to leverage the powerful capabilities of the SAP HANA database and associated technologies.

  2. What are the current technologies used for ECP? What modern technology foundation are you moving to?
    • Current technologies: SAP ECC 6.08, SAP NetWeaver 7.40 and SAP MaxDB database
    • Modern technologies: SAP S/4HANA 2023, ABAP Platform 2023, SAP HANA database

  3. When will the modernized ECP available for me? I need to know when to request budget, and resources for my own planning
    • New Customers: At the time of publication of this blog (Jan 31, 2025), all our new customers in USA, Australia and Europe are being provisioned EC Payroll on modern technology foundation. Rest of the world will follow over coming months.
    • Existing Customers: Conversion planning is in full swing. It will be a phased approach which will start in the second quarter of 2025 and will continue throughout 2027. We will reach out to customers and partners on the details of the conversion planning.

  4. Where can I find documentation to understand the difference between current and the modern technology foundation? 
    • Please use this documentation as your starting point  
  5. Where I can I find comprehensive detail of the new offering? 
    • Please refer to this Service Catalog documentation for details
  6. How long will the conversion last, when is the latest date that I will be converted?
    • We will start in second quarter 2025 with pilot customers and targeting to complete it by the end of 2027

  7. How should we plan for the conversion to modernized SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll?
    • We will publish the preliminary version of a detailed conversion guide by End-March.

  8. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered only on the modernized ECP or will it be available for current ECP version also?
    • All the new innovations and enhancements will be available ONLY on ECP built on the new technology foundation.

  9. Will the Business AI capabilities like Explain my pay statement functionality be offered on Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?
    • No. They will ONLY be offered on SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll on the modern technology foundation

  10. Who do I contact for questions?
    • Please visit this blog as this will be kept updated as more and more details become available. In the meantime, please contact your SAP account executive or partner for any questions.

entry.title: SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities: Status Update January 2025
entry.content:
The year 2025 has begun, and it is time again for an update on our SAP Continuous Influence Session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities. We had started this session in July 2023 as our single channel for collecting your suggestions for improvements related to SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

For more details, read my previous blog post New SAP Continuous Influence session for SAP S/4HANA Utilities.

As of 30 January 2025, we have received a total of 87 improvement requests (IR) in our session:

17 IRs have already been delivered since the session started. A complete list, starting with those IRs that have received most votes is shown below:

  • IR 308111 - EPRODCUST: extension of IS-U master data templates 
  • IR 321271 - BAPI_ISUPROFILE_IMPORT: EDM Import Application log  
  • IR 304589 - Full Device Replacement Reversal Process (new transaction) 
  • IR 313649 - Profilevalue status handling in formula variables 
  • IR 314904 - Change documents for EDM formula allocation 
  • IR 308110 - Mark/identify service providers as obsolete/unusable 
  • IR 331220 - IS-U billing: change the determined finale billing date 
  • IR 310707 - During device installation: automatically create MR documents for existing, upcoming bil... 
  • IR 310314 - Enable the Meter Reading Result to use the GOS (Object MTRREADDOC) 
  • IR 308652 - ICWebClient: change of IS-U contracts in web surface 
  • IR 304691 - Record the requirement for value replacement for all EDM imports 
  • IR 325261 - Include C/O and Address unique identifier in SAP ISU 
  • IR 307543 - Request Test Mode for BAPI_ISUPROFILE_IMPORT 
  • IR 307337 - Regenerate load profiles despite exitsting billings 
  • IR 306201 - SAP t-code EA22 to view Bill documents from archival call 
  • IR 304698 - Definition of profile value statuses eligible for value replacement 
  • IR 315621 - System-Id and Client in Fiori Launchpad Title

You can also view the current list of all improvement requests delivered within the SAP Continuous Influence sesison for SAP S/4HANA Utilities under https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3504/ideas-completed.

3 IRs has been accepted for development and is planned to be delivered in Q1-Q2/2025:

  • IR 338268 - EA40: no customer screen for CI fields of table ERDK 
  • IR 307906 - Fixed address is saved in CAA2 despite it is deleting in the same time in transaction BP
  • IR 338301 - AMI Services: possibility to change the proxy to EOIO queue 

2 IRs have the status "Planned (Long-Term)", which means they have been identified as being a larger investment and have been incorporated into the product roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Utilities:

  • IR 317134 - EEDM02 transaction in Fiori
  • IR 328753 - Deleting device info records en masse

2 IRs are currently under review, and 1 IR has status "Need More Info".

15 IRs have the status "For Long-term Consideration", and most of them are requesting new RAP interfaces for different entities of the SAP S/4HANA Utilities data model. 

9 IRs have the status „Acknowledged”, 5 of them have 7 or more votes and are close to being reviewed (10 votes required to do so).

37 IRs have the status “Not planned”, and we had to reject most of them either because they did not receive enough votes within twelve months or because they are out of scope for the session SAP S/4HANA Utilities. We would like to remind you that within this session dedicated to the utilities industry, we can only acknowledge and accept such IRs that are related to the utilities-industry specific part of SAP S/4HANA. For further options for influencing SAP products (beyond SAP S/4HANA Utilities) please see How Customers and Partners Can Give Input to the Roadmap of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition. A comprehensive list of all active SAP Customer Influence sessions is available here: SAP Continuous Influence Sessions - Overview.

A big thank you to all improvement request owners and other contributors for their active participation in our SAP Continuous Influence Session so far! Please continue to do so – by submitting your own ideas or by voting for improvement requests already submitted by your peers. Remember that such improvement requests that have not reached the required threshold of 10 votes within 12 months will be rejected automatically. Please be encouraged to validate and prioritize the important ones from your point of view.

Please note that a valid S user is required to participate in our (and other) SAP Continuous Influence session(s). For details see knowledge base article 3014377 - Accessing the Continuous Influence Session "SAP S/4HANA Utilities".

We appreciate your input that helps us to continuously improve SAP S/4HANA Utilities according to your business requirements.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting - New Balance Validation Process
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we will discuss about the new Balance Validation process that is delivered with S/4HANA CE2502. The following Fiori apps are part of this release:

  • Run Balance Validation and View Results
  • Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups
  • Assign Rule Group for Balance Validation

In the new Balance Validation process, you can also create a new task by using the app "Define Balance Validation Task" under Manage Your Solution (F1241) > Configure Your Solution > Application Area: Finance Sub Application Area: Corporate Close > Data Processing.

Additionally, you can import or export the rule group assignments using the following Fiori app:

  • Import/Export Validation Settings

Transition Process

The toggle 'FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION' should be switched-on to view the above apps and also to activate this new Balance Validation process. Please follow the below note for the migration process:

  • 3553668 - Group Reporting: Migration of new Balance Validation Process

The steps of the migration is explained with screenshots in the attached file of the above note.  

Is it possible to create a new Task?

Yes. You can refer to the below video that explains the creation of Balance Validation task.

As you can notice, apart from Cons. Unit and Cons. Group categories you can define a new task based on accounting. Here are the  details about Standard tasks that are delivered through content:

  • S0080 - Balance Validation in Accounting
  • S1080 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Unit Level (Reported Data)
  • S1180 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Unit Level (Standardized Data)
  • S2980 - Balance Validation at Consolidation Group Level (Consolidated Data)

How to create a Rule Group?

The rules can be defined using the app "Define Validation Rules" and those rules can be added in the Rule Group as shown below.

You can Navigate to "Define Validation Rules" from this app as shown in the below screenshot.

 

Rule Group Assignment

It is mandatory to assign the Tasks to the Rule Group. The existing Validation Methods are migrated to the rule group as explained in the SNOTE "3553668 - Group Reporting: Migration of new Balance Validation Process". You can refer to the below video that explains the Task assignment to Rule Group:

What are the steps to attach the Task to Monitor(s)?

The tasks that are created using the "Define Balance Validation Task" app can be attached to the Monitor as shown below.

It is also possible to execute the Tasks  in the app "Run Balance Validation and View Results" as shown in the below screenshot.

How is the new Solution better than the old one?

The new Balance Validation process has the following benefits:

  • You can define a new task by choosing one of the Task Categories and run the rule definitions either in Group Reporting or in Accounting as a data source.
  • The performance is optimized and the memory consumption has relatively improved
  • It is now possible to define a maximum of 6 Group By fields
  • All Balance Validation tasks can be executed in one app: Run Balance Validation and View Results. 

Concluding with Additional Links

Please feel free to share your thoughts by commenting on this blog. Also, we can use this blog as a communication tool for any clarification that you require on this topic. You can also follow my profile for more content on analytical application and Balance Validation for group reporting. I would recommend posting your questions by specifying the tag: “SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting”

Define Balance Validation Tasks | SAP Help Portal

Balance Validation Tasks and Task Categories | SAP Help Portal

Working with Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups | SAP Help Portal

Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups | SAP Help Portal

Assign Rule Groups for Balance Validation | SAP Help Portal

Run Balance Validation and View Results | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Change Management in SAP S/4HANA-Transformationen: Erkenntnisse aus meiner Masterarbeit
entry.content:
Warum Change Management im SAP S/4HANA-Kontext entscheidend ist

SAP S/4HANA-Transformationen sind weit mehr als reine IT-Projekte – sie bedeuten tiefgreifende organisatorische Veränderungen. Dennoch konzentrieren sich viele Unternehmen primär auf technologische Aspekte und unterschätzen die menschliche Dimension des Wandels. Doch gerade hier entscheidet sich der Projekterfolg: Ohne Akzeptanz und Unterstützung der Mitarbeitenden kann selbst die beste technologische Lösung scheitern.

Im Rahmen meiner Masterarbeit habe ich die Rolle des Organizational Change Management in SAP S/4HANA-Transformationen untersucht. Dazu wurden eine Umfrage unter 98 Fachkräften sowie fünf Experteninterviews durchgeführt. Die wichtigsten Erkenntnisse und praxisnahen Handlungsempfehlungen stelle ich in diesem Beitrag vor.

Kurzüberblick: Daten aus Umfrage und Experteninterviews

Die Umfrage umfasste 98 Fachkräfte, die aktiv an SAP S/4HANA-Transformationen beteiligt waren. Die Mehrheit stammte aus Deutschland (64,3 %), gefolgt von China (18,4 %) und den USA (13,3 %). Die Befragten waren überwiegend zwischen 25 und 54 Jahre alt, und mehr als die Hälfte arbeitete in Unternehmen mit über 500 Mitarbeitenden. Während 43 % keine Führungsverantwortung hatten, waren 39 % in leitenden Positionen tätig.

Ergänzend dazu wurden fünf erfahrene Change-Management-Experten befragt – zwei aus den USA und drei aus Deutschland. Sie teilten wertvolle Einblicke aus der Praxis und beschrieben die häufigsten Herausforderungen sowie Erfolgsfaktoren in Transformationsprojekten.

Wichtige Erkenntnisse aus der Analyse

1. Kommunikation ist der wichtigste Erfolgsfaktor

Kommunikation ist das Herzstück jeder erfolgreichen Transformation. Die Befragung zeigt, dass eine klare, frühzeitige und kontinuierliche Kommunikation der wichtigste Erfolgsfaktor ist. Entscheidend sind:

  • Frühzeitige Kommunikation über die Notwendigkeit und den Nutzen der Veränderung.
  • Zielgruppenorientierte Ansprache, um Unsicherheiten abzubauen.
  • Interaktive Formate wie Workshops und Q&A-Sessions, um den Dialog zu fördern.

2. Führungskräfte als Schlüsselakteure

Ein oft unterschätzter Faktor: Führungskräfte sind mehr als nur Informationsvermittler – sie sind entscheidende Treiber der Veränderung. Die Studie zeigt, dass Unternehmen erfolgreicher sind, wenn Führungskräfte aktiv:

  • die Vision der Transformation vermitteln,
  • als Vorbilder agieren und den Wandel selbst mittragen,
  • Hindernisse beseitigen und Ressourcen bereitstellen.

3. Schulung und Weiterbildung statt Überforderung

Ein häufiger Fehler: Mitarbeitende werden nicht ausreichend auf neue Prozesse vorbereitet. Change Management erfordert neben technischen Schulungen auch Trainings zu neuen Prozessen und Rollenveränderungen. Effektive Maßnahmen beinhalten:

  • Individuell zugeschnittene Schulungsprogramme für verschiedene Stakeholdergruppen.
  • Digitale Lernplattformen wie „WalkMe“ oder „SAP Enable Now“ zur Begleitung des Lernprozesses.
  • Praxistrainings, die frühzeitig Einblicke in die neue Systemlandschaft ermöglichen.

4. Klare Ziele und Visionen treiben die Transformation voran

Ein entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor ist eine starke, inspirierende Vision. Unternehmen, die klare Ziele formulieren und diese aktiv kommunizieren, schaffen es besser, ihre Mitarbeitenden zu motivieren und den Wandel nachhaltig zu gestalten. Dazu gehören:

  • Klare Roadmaps und Meilensteine, die den Fortschritt greifbar machen.
  • Regelmäßige Erfolgskontrollen, um sicherzustellen, dass die Transformation auf Kurs bleibt.
  • Einbindung der Mitarbeitenden in die Zielsetzung, um Identifikation mit der Veränderung zu fördern.

5. Die am häufigsten genannten Change-Management-Methoden

Die Analyse zeigt: Ein strukturierter Change-Management-Ansatz ist essenziell. Zwei Methoden wurden in der Befragung besonders häufig genannt:

  • SAP Activate Methodology: Eine iterative, prozessorientierte Herangehensweise, die speziell für SAP-Implementierungen entwickelt wurde.
  • ADKAR-Modell von PROSCI: Eine bewährte Methode, die den individuellen Veränderungsprozess der Mitarbeitenden in den Mittelpunkt stellt.

Wichtig ist die flexible Anpassung der Methodik an Unternehmensbedürfnisse.

6. Change Management ist ein Marathon, kein Sprint

Ein häufiger Trugschluss ist, Change Management als einmalige Maßnahme zu betrachten. Erfolgreiche Unternehmen begleiten den Wandel kontinuierlich durch:

  • Change-Readiness-Analysen, um Widerstände frühzeitig zu erkennen.
  • Regelmäßige Feedbackschleifen, um die Strategie bei Bedarf anzupassen.
  • Langfristige Maßnahmen, um die Nachhaltigkeit des Wandels zu sichern.

7. Erfolgsmomente sichtbar machen

Veränderungen sind oft herausfordernd – deshalb ist es entscheidend, auch kleine Erfolge zu feiern. Unternehmen, die Erfolge bewusst hervorheben, steigern die Motivation und das Engagement ihrer Mitarbeitenden. Besonders wirksam sind laut der Befragung:

  • Team-Events & Feiern, um Fortschritte gemeinsam zu würdigen.
  • Individuelle Anreize, die den Beitrag der Mitarbeitenden honorieren.

Von Erkenntnissen zu Maßnahmen: Wie Unternehmen Change Management erfolgreich umsetzen

Die gewonnenen Erkenntnisse zeigen, dass Change Management kein optionaler Bestandteil von SAP S/4HANA-Transformationen ist, sondern ein entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor. Unternehmen können diese Erkenntnisse gezielt in die Praxis umsetzen, indem sie:

  • Frühzeitig eine Change-Strategie definieren und mit der technischen Planung abstimmen.
  • Kommunikation nicht als einmalige Maßnahme, sondern als kontinuierlichen Prozess betrachten.
  • Führungskräfte gezielt in ihre Rolle als Change-Leader entwickeln und sie aktiv als Sponsoren einsetzen.
  • Stakeholder-Engagement intensivieren, indem sie regelmäßige Feedbackrunden mit betroffenen Mitarbeitenden und Führungskräften durchführen.
  • Agile Change-Management-Modelle nutzen, um die Strategie kontinuierlich an neue Herausforderungen anzupassen.
  • Change Agents einbinden, um den Wandel auf allen Unternehmensebenen zu fördern.
  • Schulungen praxisnah und iterativ gestalten, um Mitarbeitende schrittweise auf neue Prozesse vorzubereiten.
  • Monitoring und Erfolgsmessung verstärken, indem KPIs definiert werden, um Fortschritte und Akzeptanz der Transformation messbar zu machen.
  • Nachhaltige Unterstützung sicherstellen, indem begleitende Maßnahmen auch nach der Go-Live-Phase angeboten werden.

Diese Maßnahmen helfen nicht nur, Change Management erfolgreich zu verankern, sondern auch die langfristige Akzeptanz und Effektivität der Transformation sicherzustellen.

Fazit: Change Management als entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor

Die Ergebnisse dieser Masterarbeit zeigen eines ganz deutlich: Der Erfolg einer SAP S/4HANA-Transformation steht und fällt mit dem Change Management. Technologie allein bringt keine nachhaltige Veränderung – der Schlüssel liegt in den Menschen, die sie nutzen.

Ein durchdachter Change-Management-Ansatz, eine klare Kommunikation und eine starke Führung sind essenziell, um Mitarbeitende nicht nur durch den Wandel zu führen, sondern sie aktiv einzubinden. Denn eine Transformation ist dann erfolgreich, wenn sie nicht nur umgesetzt, sondern auch von den Menschen im Unternehmen gelebt wird.

Ich freue mich auf Ihre Gedanken und Erfahrungen zu diesem Thema! Teilen Sie gerne Ihre Perspektiven oder stellen Sie Fragen in den Kommentaren. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam darüber diskutieren, wie Change Management in der Praxis noch effektiver gestaltet werden kann!

Liebe Grüße, Sandra

entry.title: Streamlining direct material sourcing from Design to Contract- Secure collaboration
entry.content:
Engineering changes can be a profit killer. Delays can devastate time to market. The answer? Strategic partnerships with direct material suppliers who are engaged early in product design. Collaboration prevents rework, accelerates time to market and safeguards your bottom line. Without it, companies risk escalating costs, lost revenue and damaged reputations.

Manufacturers need a robust and collaborative direct sourcing platform to fully realize these benefits. It must enable intuitive, context-driven collaboration between internal teams (R&D, project management, manufacturing, quality) and supplier’s stakeholders, with ironclad security for sensitive business data.

SAP Ariba Direct Material Sourcing delivers this critical capability, streamlining sourcing from design to contract. The SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release enhances this further with native integration into SAP Integrated Product Development, enabling secure collaboration on engineering drawings and specifications—ultimately driving higher profit margins

Here is how the new capability works:

  1. Create secure internal collaboration by inviting cross functional experts to finalise the designs and specifications which needs to be shared with suppliers who gets invited into the sourcing process

 

 

2. Standard workflow which orchestrates finalisation of the drawings and specifications in the collaboration and notifies sourcing manager via status update to publish the sourcing project to invited suppliers

 

 

 

 

3. Invited suppliers can securely access the shared drawings and specifications via SAP Supplier quotation management (Supplier application) to fully understand the design requirements of the requested direct materials

 

4. Supplier’s can use markup for any clarifications which they need from buyer’s organisation

 

 

5. Right stakeholder from buyer’s organization provides clarifications intime to supplier and system takes care of keeping all the discussion intact

 

 

 

6. Supplier creates and submit the best quotation with the complete understanding of the requirements associated with every material quoted in the quotation

7. Sourcing manager along with other expert stakeholders reviews the supplier quotation to finalize the award decision. Buying organization stakeholders can use markups to clarify supplier response.

This new capability delivers significant benefits:

  • Faster Time-to-Market: Streamlined collaboration and automated workflows accelerate design finalization and sourcing processes.
  • Reduced Costs: Early collaboration prevents costly engineering changes and rework.
  • Improved Supplier Engagement: Secure access and clear communication enable suppliers to submit accurate and competitive bids.
  • Enhanced Quality: Thorough design reviews and clear specifications ensure high-quality products.
  • Increased Collaboration Efficiency: Secure platform fosters seamless collaboration between internal teams and suppliers.

entry.title: Kit To Stock in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Warehouse management is at the core of efficient supply chain operations, and SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) plays a pivotal role in streamlining these processes. Among its many features, the Kit-to-Stock process stands out as a crucial mechanism for assembling products (kits) from individual components and stocking them for future use. This blog explores the purpose, process, and benefits of Kit-to-Stock in SAP EWM, with a practical example to bring the concept to life.

The Kit-to-Stock process involves assembling a final product (referred to as the Kit Header) from individual materials or parts (called Kit Components) and storing the assembled kit in the warehouse. This ensures that the product is ready for future use, whether it’s for fulfilling customer orders or internal production needs.

A Practical Example

Let’s break this down using a real-world scenario:

  • Kit Header: EV Charging Port Assembly
  • Kit Components: Charging Port, Connector Cable

In this scenario, the EV Charging Port Assembly (Kit Header) is the finished product created by combining components such as the Charging Port and Connector Cable. These components are picked from the warehouse, assembled, and stocked as a single unit.

Benefits of the Kit-to-Stock Process

  • Operational Efficiency: The process saves time by pre-assembling kits, reducing the need to handle individual components repeatedly.
  • Inventory Accuracy: Real-time tracking of kits and components ensures precise inventory management.
  • Flexibility: The process adapts to various scenarios, including bulk kit assembly for high-demand periods.

The Kit-to-Stock process in SAP EWM is a game-changer for warehouse management. By streamlining the assembly and stocking of kits, it enhances efficiency, reduces errors, and ensures readiness for customer demands or internal requirements. Whether you’re assembling EV Charging Ports or any other kits, this process offers the flexibility and accuracy modern warehouses need to stay competitive.

Let’s dive into configuration to make this scenario work.

Configuration:

1.   Define Order Type for VAS for KIT to stock


2.   Define Storage type for Work Center

Create a bin (KITS_BIN1) in Transaction /N/SCWM/LS01 and generate an activity area (KITS) for the specified storage type using transaction /N/SCWM/GENA. Additionally, perform bin sorting for the activity area using /N/SCWM/SBST. These steps have been omitted from this blog to maintain brevity.

3.   Process- Oriented Storage Control (POSC)

The Process-Oriented Storage Control (POSC) in the kitting process is configured to manage both key aspects of the operation.

Inbound Process: POSC applies to the kitting header product, ensuring that products are moved from the kitting work center and put away into their final storage bin, making them available for sales.

Outbound Process: POSC is configured to manage the picking of kitting components from the storage area and transferring them to the kitting work center.

External Storage Process Steps

Storage Process- Definition

Assign Storage Process step

Process-Oriented Storage Control

4.   Work Center

A Work Center in the warehouse is a designated area for performing specific physical activities, replicated in SAP EWM to reflect the actual workflow. This setup allows the system to recognize the activities carried out at the Work Center, enabling warehouse associates to update information directly into the system.

In the context of the kitting process, this Work Center differs from a regular work center. Since the kitting process involves both inbound and outbound operations configured in the POSC, the storage type associated with this Work Center has the role “I - Work Center with Staging Area Group.”(Refer Step-2). This role is essential as it facilitates posting of the Goods Issue for consuming kitting components and the Goods Receipt for receiving the kitting header product.

Define Work Center

SAP provides a standard Work Center Layout, "KITS," which can be utilized for the Kitting Work Center. This layout will be assigned to the external step defined in the POSC configuration.

Assign bin to work center- /N/SCWM/TWORKST

5.   Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR)

The WOCR is essential for triggering POSC during the outbound process. In this scenario, the WOCR configuration also enables the automatic creation of Pick HUs. While WOCR offers a range of functionalities, this specific scenario focuses on utilizing the Packing Profile to facilitate the automatic Pick HU process.

  • Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation

For Auto Pick HU, There are more configuration to be done, listed down the steps but screenshots are omitted. if you are interested please refer the following link for detailed Configuration on WOCR
Link: https://www.stelliteworks.com/warehouse-order-creation-rule-wocr-in-sap-ewm-for-picking/

  • Packaging Specification with “0BDL- Work Order Creation” in transaction /N/SCWM/PACKSPEC.( Refer Step-11)
  • Assign Additional Attributes to Activity Area in transaction /N/SCWM/SEBA.
  • Define Warehouse Process Type for Pick HU with Internal Movement.
  • Assign pick HU WPT in Warehouse Number Control.

Define Creation Rule for Warehouse Order

Assign Outbound Storage Process which helps in triggering POSC in Outbound Process and assign Packing profile for auto pick HU.

6.   Warehouse Process Type

Warehouse Process Types need both inbound and outbound side and this also is used to trigger POSC in Inbound process.

  • Define Warehouse Process Type

Determine Warehouse Process Type

7.   Define Put away Strategies for Header Product after Kitting

Put away Strategies to put the finished product after kitting.

  • Define Storage type search sequence for put away

  • Assign Storage type to the Storage type search Sequence

  • Define Put away Control Indicator

  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

8.    Stock Removal Strategies

Stock Removal Strategies to pick the components from storage area to  Kitting Work Center

  • Define Storage type search sequence for stock removal

  • Define Control Indicator for Search Sequence
  • Specify Storage type search sequence for Put away

9.   Bill Of Materials (BOM)

 10.  Packaging Specification

Pack Specification detailed configuration is skipped in this blog.

 

11.   Product Master

Header:

Components:

 

The Kit-to-Stock process is an integral part of Value-Added Services (VAS) in SAP EWM. The process begins with the creation and maintenance of VAS for Kit-to-Stock.

Maintain VAS- Kit to Stock- /N/SCWM/VAS_KTS

SAP provides the flexibility to create kits with or without a Bill of Materials (BOM)

  • With BOM: Utilizes an existing BOM already configured in the system.
  • Without BOM: Enables the addition of components directly to the header product.

In cases where BOMs are used, they are created using CS01, as shown in Step 9. The configuration involves assigning the order type, packaging specification, and the header product along with its quantity. This setup allows the creation of the VAS for Kit-to-Stock, as illustrated in Steps 1, 10, and 11 respectively.

Outbound and Inbound Deliveries in the Kitting Process

The Kit-to-Stock process involves both Outbound and Inbound deliveries

  • Outbound Delivery: Created to pick the components required for kitting from storage bins and transfer them to the kitting work center.
  • Inbound Delivery: Created in the header details of the kit to facilitate the put away of the final product back into storage bins after the kitting process.

 

Outbound & Inbound Deliveries

Outbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDO

The Outbound Delivery created manually as see indicator in the image and it includes two line items representing the components required to form the kit. These line items are configured with the warehouse process type "KTSO", as per Step 6. Outbound delivery is to create picking warehouse tasks for picking the components from storage bins and transferring them to the kitting work center.

 

  • Inbound Delivery- /N/SCWM/PRDI

Inbound Delivery (Warehouse Request) is automatically created to facilitate the movement of the final product, assembled through the kitting process, to the designated storage bin. This ensures the product is ready for sale and can be efficiently shipped when an order is placed.

The Inbound Delivery contains only one line item, representing the final product. This line item is created with the warehouse process type “KTSI”, as illustrated in the image below. This process ensures seamless integration of the kitting output into the warehouse workflow, enabling quick availability for shipping.

 Creating Picking Warehouse Task for components for kitting

To create warehouse tasks for picking manually, the system selects the products from the ET10 storage type, as shown in the image below. These products are available in the bin ET10-10-14-04, which belongs to the ET10 storage type. The picking process is driven by the Storage Type Search Sequence for stock removal, configured in Step 8, ensuring that the system efficiently identifies and retrieves the required products.

Upon confirmation of the warehouse task, the products are transferred to the kitting work center bin KTS_BIN1. However, the system generated an additional warehouse task due to the configuration in Step 5, where auto pick HU was enabled. To proceed, filter the warehouse tasks using the process type “3070” and open the warehouse tasks in the monitor due to the configuration in Step-3.

Only one warehouse task was found because both products were picked in the same pick HU and moved together to KTS_BIN1. Upon confirmation, the products are transferred to the work center as shown in the below image. At the work center, associates perform the kitting process and update the system to reflect the completion of the activity.

Confirmation of VAS Order (Kit To Stock)-/N/SCWM/VASEXEC

 The products, along with their bin and VAS order details, can be viewed in this transaction. Under the Post Components section, if the HU is posted using the Post HU button, the system will perform a goods issue for those products in the outbound delivery, indicating that the products have been consumed for the kitting process.

Now Let’s pack them in new HU for processing

The system navigates to the packing work center, where the final product is packed into a new HU, as shown below. After packing, save the changes, and upon returning to the previous screen, the newly packed HU will be listed in the kitting work center bin.

When the newly packed HU containing the final product is posted by clicking the Post HU button, a Goods Receipt is recorded, indicating that the final product has been successfully created in the system.

Next, click on “Complete Process Step for HU” and save the changes. The system will automatically create a warehouse task (52273) to move the product from the work center to the designated storage bin, initiating the put away process.

A warehouse task is created to move the product from KTS_BIN1 to the E101-04-03 bin. This bin serves as the final storage location for the kit header product. Upon confirmation, the put away process will also be completed in the inbound delivery.

Let’s check the bin for final product

Finally, the kit product is available for sale with stock type F2. When an order for this product is received, it can be directly picked from the designated bin and shipped.

entry.title: Industry 5.0: The Next Revolution in Manufacturing
entry.content:
Introduction

The industrial landscape is undergoing a seismic shift. As we transition from the hyper-automated, data-driven era of Industry 4.0 to the human-centric, sustainable, and resilient framework of Industry 5.0, businesses face both unprecedented challenges and opportunities. At the heart of this evolution lies the integration of advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and collaborative robots (cobots) with human creativity and ethical practices. In this blog, we explore how SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, is uniquely positioned to drive this transformation, enabling organizations to thrive in the Industry 5.0 era.

Understanding Industry 5.0

Industry 5.0 represents a shift from the automation-centric approach of Industry 4.0 to a more human-centric model. It focuses on leveraging the strengths of both humans and machines to create a symbiotic relationship. This collaboration enables more personalized and flexible production processes, catering to the unique needs and preferences of individual customers.

Key characteristics of Industry 5.0 include:

  1. Human-Machine Collaboration: Unlike Industry 4.0, which primarily focused on automation, Industry 5.0 emphasizes the collaboration between humans and machines. This synergy allows for more creative and innovative solutions.

  2. Personalization: Industry 5.0 enables mass customization, where products are tailored to individual customer preferences. This personalization is achieved through the integration of advanced technologies and human insights.

  3. Sustainability: With a growing emphasis on environmental responsibility, Industry 5.0 aims to create more sustainable manufacturing processes. This includes reducing waste, conserving resources, and minimizing the environmental impact of production.

  4. Resilience: The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need for resilient supply chains. Industry 5.0 focuses on building robust and adaptable manufacturing systems that can withstand disruptions and quickly recover from unforeseen events.

The Evolution of Industrial Revolutions

To fully appreciate the significance of Industry 5.0, it's essential to understand the evolution of industrial revolutions. The first industrial revolution, which began in the late 18th century, was characterized by the mechanization of production using water and steam power. This period saw the introduction of machines that replaced manual labor, significantly increasing productivity.

The second industrial revolution, in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, brought about mass production and assembly lines. This era was marked by the widespread use of electricity and the development of new manufacturing processes, leading to the rise of large-scale industries.

The third industrial revolution, starting in the late 20th century, introduced digital technologies and automation. This period saw the adoption of computers, robotics, and information technology, transforming manufacturing processes and enabling greater efficiency and precision.

Industry 4.0, which emerged in the early 21st century, focused on the integration of digital technologies and the Internet of Things (IoT). This era emphasized automation, data exchange, and the use of cyber-physical systems to create smart factories. Industry 4.0 technologies, such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and big data analytics, have revolutionized manufacturing processes, enabling real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and enhanced operational efficiency.

The Shift to Industry 5.0

While Industry 4.0 has brought about significant advancements, it has also highlighted the limitations of a purely automation-centric approach. The focus on automation and digital technologies often overlooks the unique strengths and creativity of human workers. Industry 5.0 aims to address this gap by fostering a more collaborative relationship between humans and machines.

In Industry 5.0, humans and machines work together, leveraging their respective strengths to create innovative solutions. This collaboration enables more personalized and flexible production processes, catering to the unique needs and preferences of individual customers. By integrating human insights with advanced technologies, Industry 5.0 aims to create a more sustainable and resilient manufacturing landscape.

The Role of SAP in Industry 5.0

SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, is poised to play a pivotal role in the transition to Industry 5.0. With its comprehensive suite of solutions, SAP can help businesses navigate the complexities of this new industrial era and unlock unprecedented value.

  1. Intelligent Technologies: SAP's intelligent technologies, such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things (IoT), enable real-time data analysis and predictive insights. These technologies facilitate human-machine collaboration, allowing for more informed decision-making and enhanced operational efficiency.

  2. Customer Experience: SAP's Customer Experience (CX) solutions help businesses understand and anticipate customer needs. By leveraging data analytics and personalization tools, companies can deliver tailored products and services, enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

  3. Sustainable Operations: SAP's sustainability solutions support businesses in achieving their environmental goals. From carbon footprint tracking to waste management, SAP provides the tools needed to create more sustainable manufacturing processes.

  4. Supply Chain Resilience: SAP's supply chain management solutions help businesses build resilient and adaptable supply chains. By integrating real-time data and predictive analytics, companies can quickly respond to disruptions and ensure business continuity.

  5. Human-Centric Design: SAP's human-centric design principles focus on creating user-friendly and intuitive solutions. By prioritizing the user experience, SAP enables seamless collaboration between humans and machines, fostering innovation and creativity.

Personalizing the Manufacturing Experience

One of the most exciting aspects of Industry 5.0 is the ability to personalize the manufacturing experience. In today's fast-paced world, customers demand products that are tailored to their unique needs and preferences. Industry 5.0 enables mass customization, where products are designed and manufactured to meet individual customer requirements.

SAP's solutions play a crucial role in enabling this personalization. By leveraging data analytics and customer insights, businesses can gain a deeper understanding of customer preferences and behaviors. This information can be used to create personalized products and services, enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

For example, a clothing manufacturer can use SAP's solutions to analyze customer data and identify trends and preferences. This information can be used to design and produce customized clothing items that meet the unique needs of individual customers. By integrating human insights with advanced technologies, the manufacturer can create a more personalized and flexible production process, catering to the diverse needs of its customer base.

Enhancing Sustainability in Manufacturing

Sustainability is a critical aspect of Industry 5.0. With a growing emphasis on environmental responsibility, businesses are increasingly focused on creating more sustainable manufacturing processes. SAP's solutions support businesses in achieving their sustainability goals, from reducing waste and conserving resources to minimizing the environmental impact of production.

For instance, a food manufacturer can use SAP's sustainability solutions to track and manage its carbon footprint. By analyzing data on energy consumption, waste generation, and resource usage, the manufacturer can identify areas for improvement and implement sustainable practices. This not only helps in reducing the environmental impact of production but also enhances the manufacturer's reputation as a responsible and sustainable business.

Building Resilient Supply Chains

The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of resilient supply chains. Industry 5.0 focuses on building robust and adaptable manufacturing systems that can withstand disruptions and quickly recover from unforeseen events. SAP's supply chain management solutions play a crucial role in enabling this resilience.

By integrating real-time data and predictive analytics, businesses can gain visibility into their supply chains and identify potential disruptions. This information can be used to develop contingency plans and ensure business continuity. For example, a pharmaceutical company can use SAP's solutions to monitor its supply chain and identify potential disruptions in the supply of raw materials. By developing alternative sourcing strategies and building inventory buffers, the company can ensure a steady supply of essential medicines, even in the face of disruptions.

Fostering Innovation and Creativity

Innovation and creativity are at the heart of Industry 5.0. By fostering a collaborative relationship between humans and machines, Industry 5.0 enables the development of innovative solutions that address complex challenges. SAP's solutions support businesses in fostering this innovation and creativity.

For instance, an automotive manufacturer can use SAP's intelligent technologies to analyze data on vehicle performance and identify areas for improvement. By collaborating with human experts, the manufacturer can develop innovative solutions that enhance vehicle efficiency, safety, and performance. This not only helps in creating better products but also fosters a culture of innovation and creativity within the organization.

The Human Touch in Industry 5.0

While advanced technologies play a crucial role in Industry 5.0, the human touch remains essential. Industry 5.0 emphasizes the importance of human insights, creativity, and decision-making in the manufacturing process. SAP's human-centric design principles focus on creating user-friendly and intuitive solutions that enable seamless collaboration between humans and machines.

For example, a healthcare provider can use SAP's solutions to analyze patient data and identify trends and patterns. By collaborating with human experts, the provider can develop personalized treatment plans that cater to the unique needs of individual patients. This not only enhances patient outcomes but also fosters a more compassionate and human-centric approach to healthcare.

Conclusion: Embracing the Future of Manufacturing

Industry 5.0 marks a significant evolution in the manufacturing landscape, emphasizing human-machine collaboration, personalization, sustainability, and resilience. SAP, with its comprehensive suite of intelligent technologies and customer-centric solutions, is well-positioned to drive this transformation. By embracing Industry 5.0 principles and leveraging SAP's innovative solutions, businesses can unlock new opportunities, enhance operational efficiency, and create a more sustainable future.

As we step into this new industrial era, the collaboration between humans and machines will redefine manufacturing processes, leading to unprecedented levels of innovation and productivity. With SAP as a transforming force, businesses can navigate the complexities of Industry 5.0 and thrive in this rapidly evolving landscape.

entry.title: Introduction to SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales is a robust solution designed to streamline and optimize the sales processes between affiliated companies within a corporate group. This functionality is particularly useful for organizations where a local sales office sells products to external customers, but the goods are delivered directly from a producing company or a central warehouse. The process ensures seamless integration between sales, procurement, delivery, and billing operations, enabling efficient intercompany transactions.

Business Process Overview

 

The Advanced Intercompany Sales process involves the following key steps:

  1. Sales Order Creation: A standard sales order is created by the selling company for an external customer. The system automatically identifies if the delivering plant belongs to a different company code, making the order relevant for intercompany processing.
  2. Intercompany Purchase Order: Upon saving the sales order, the system generates an intercompany purchase order in the selling company. This purchase order is directed to the delivering company, which is responsible for supplying the goods.
  3. Intercompany Sales Order: The delivering company receives the purchase order and creates an intercompany sales order. This order is used to manage the internal transaction between the delivering company and the selling company.
  4. Delivery and Goods Movement: The delivering company processes the outbound delivery and ships the goods directly to the external customer. The system manages the goods movements, including the transfer of goods into and out of stock in transit, ensuring accurate inventory tracking.
  5. Billing: Two types of invoices are generated:
    • Customer Invoice: The selling company bills the external customer for the goods delivered.
    • Intercompany Customer Invoice: The delivering company bills the selling company for the intercompany transaction.
  6. Invoice Verification: The intercompany supplier invoice is verified against the intercompany customer invoice to ensure accuracy and compliance.

Key Features

  • Real-Time Integration: The process is fully integrated into the SAP S/4HANA value chain monitoring framework, allowing real-time tracking and monitoring of each step.
  • Valuated Stock in Transit: The system uses valuated stock in transit to manage the transfer of goods between affiliated companies, ensuring accurate financial reporting and inventory management.
  • Automated Workflows: Many steps, such as the creation of intercompany purchase orders and sales orders, are automated, reducing manual effort and minimizing errors.

Setup Steps

To enable Advanced Intercompany Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, the following setup steps are required:

  1. Enable the Process: Activate the Advanced Intercompany Sales process (scope item 5D2) through expert configuration. This involves enabling specific pre-delivered configurations.
  2. Assign Purchasing Organization and Group: Assign a purchasing organization and purchasing group to the sales organization of the intercompany sales order.
  3. Configure Pricing: Set up pricing independently for the delivering and selling companies. This includes defining pricing procedures and condition types for internal and external customers.
  4. Define Transfer of Control Dates: Configure the internal and external transfer of control dates to manage the timing of goods movements and revenue recognition.
  5. Monitor Value Chains: Use the Monitor Value Chains app to orchestrate and monitor each step of the intercompany sales process.

Analyzing the Process

To analyze and resolve issues in the Advanced Intercompany Sales process, you can use the following tools:

  • Sales Order Fulfillment - Analyze and Resolve Issues App: This app helps identify and resolve issues that may impede the fulfillment of sales orders. It provides insights into critical stages of the process and facilitates collaboration with relevant contacts.
  • Monitor Value Chains App: This app offers a comprehensive view of the entire intercompany sales process, allowing you to track the status of sales orders, deliveries, and invoices.

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Intercompany Sales provides a comprehensive solution for managing complex intercompany sales processes. By automating key steps and integrating them into a unified framework, it enhances efficiency, accuracy, and visibility across the supply chain. Proper setup and configuration, along with the use of analytical tools, ensure smooth operation and timely resolution of any issues that may arise.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 6 – Preventive or Recurring Service Processing
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog:

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 6:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the service contract and the maintenance plan. On scheduling the maintenance plan, both the documents are created simultaneously i.e. service order with execution order item and billable maintenance order along with the linkage between the two.

Maintenance plans enable the scheduling of recurring services at predefined intervals. These services can be part of routine maintenance on a periodic basis or triggered based on usage or performance.

For e.g. A car dealership offers preventive maintenance services for customers who have purchased vehicles from them. As part of the service agreement, the dealership provides regular doorstep maintenance every 6 months, to ensure optimal vehicle performance and longevity. A time-based maintenance plan can be set up for the car. After every 6 months, the maintenance plan triggers the creation of two orders:

  • A Service Order (the commercial object).
  • A Maintenance Order (the execution object with task list).

The service order and the maintenance order are linked with each other through document flow.

In addition to the routine maintenance, the dealership also recommends a battery replacement every 2 years. This service can be planned with a strategy-based maintenance plan.

Creation of Service Contract

A service contract can be created using ‘Manage Service Contracts’ App.
The following details could be filled while creating a service contract:

  • Sold-to-Party
  • Organizational data such as Sales Organization
  • Price information such as price agreement
  • Parties involved
  • Item details such as billing plan, price information, object list, product list etc.

Creation of Maintenance Plan

A maintenance plan can be created using ‘Add Single Plan’, ‘Add Strategy Controlled Plan’ or ‘Add Multiple Counter Plan’ Apps to create time-based or performance-based single cycle plans, strategy plans or multiple counter maintenance plans respectively.

The following details could be filled while creating a maintenance plan:

  1. Select the maintenance plan category.
  2. On the Item tab, enter a new item by specifying item description and technical object. In the planning data section enter service contract, service contract item, and order type. The service contract is mandatory.
  3. Provide a name for the maintenance plan and enter the cycle details. Optionally, enter a task list.
  4. Enter the scheduling period under maintenance plan scheduling parameters tab to specify the number of days, months, or years over which scheduling will take place.
  5. The ‘Do Not Rel. Immediately’ checkbox can be marked, if the maintenance order must not be released immediately once created.

Scheduling of Maintenance Plan

Once a maintenance plan is created, the next step is to schedule it. In the ‘Schedule Maintenance Plan' App, do the following:

  1. Enter the plan number created.
  2. When Start is clicked, the scheduling list gets populated with all scheduled calls and the dates for which they have been scheduled.
  3. Once Save is clicked, depending on the call dates, the service order and its corresponding maintenance order is generated as call objects.

The service product in the service order is copied from the service contract as the execution order item. The item category for this service product is determined from the ‘Copy Item Categories - Item Category Determination’ customizing.

Similarly, the Sold-to-Party in the service order is also taken over from the service contract.

While creating the service order and the maintenance order, the order type mapping configurations (SPRO -> Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration-> Map Order Types) are not relevant. The service transaction type and plant maintenance order type are user inputs in the maintenance plan.

The operations and components in the maintenance order are derived from the task list entered in the maintenance plan.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

                                                      Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

 

Release of Execution Order Item

Once the service order and execution order item details are finalized, the lifecycle status can be set to ‘Released’. The execution status on the execution order item is ‘Execution Planning’ and the maintenance order status is 'CRTD' (Created).

Note: The execution order item can be rejected only when the execution status is ‘Execution Planning’.

The document flow between the service order and maintenance order can be viewed in Transaction History assignment block. The maintenance plan and maintenance item document flow can also be viewed here.

The details of the maintenance order connected to the execution order item can be viewed in the Maintenance Order assignment block in the item.

Release of Maintenance Order

Once the maintenance order is created from the service order, the maintenance order is in planning phase. After the planning is done, the maintenance order is set to ‘Released’ by the Maintenance Planner. Then the execution of service can be started. The maintenance order status is changed from ‘CRTD’ to ‘REL’ (Released). With this status transition, the execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘In Execution’.

Completion of Execution Order Item

After the execution of service is completed by the Maintenance Technician, the maintenance order status is set to ‘TECO’ (Technically Completed).  With this status transition, the lifecycle status of execution order item is set to ‘Completed’. The execution status on the execution order item is set to ‘Execution Done’.

Once the service order is set to ‘Completed’, the completion date is updated for the call in maintenance plan scheduling list.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the creation service order and maintenance order from maintenance plan. As we proceed with the execution of maintenance order, the lifecycle and execution status of execution order item in service order are in continuous alignment with the status of maintenance order at every step.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: Staffing Facet in Project Control - Professional Services Projects App (F6869)
entry.content:
Great news! You no longer need to open another app to view the staffing details for your projects. Until now you had to use the Staffing Analysis app for these details. All this information is now available within the Project Control - Professional Services Projects app.

We've introduced information about project staffing within the app Project Control - Professional Services Projects. The Staffing facet provides comprehensive information related to resource requests. This includes work package details, work items, roles, requested and staffed efforts, worker and staffing details, delivery organization, billing control category, and work package type. Additional details such as costing data and billing element details are also available.

For example, you can highlight if staffing has been cost effective by comparing the costs that you had planned and the staffing cost of the resource.

Another new feature is that you can group the staffing for different billing elements which allows you to see who is working on which contract items. (for example, fixed price and T&M)

 

This facet also supports staffing done within external staffing solutions like SAP Project and Resource Management (6JO). If there are multiple workers staffed to a resource request, you can easily view the breakdown of the staffed efforts and the staffed resource organizations.

If you have not been using the app Project Control – Professional Services Projects, please take a look at it on your home page, on the Fiori launchpad.

Here are few additional details:

 Fiori App Ref library: https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6869')/S34

App Help :   https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/db25b660143d4ffbad8e7f4825b9d83e.html?locale=en-US

 If you have additional feedback on the app, please submit them here. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/930

entry.title: A closer look at Event based Production accounting in UPA
entry.content:
UPA and the change in Production accounting from month end to Realtime 

Unless you have been living under a rock you must have already heard of the new Universal Parallel accounting that SAP has already released and is generally available for customers.

I have been working in the SAP Controlling area since the last two decades. The onset of S/4 HANA brought in some changes relating to cost elements, allocations Actual costing and material ledger. However, the production and costing side remained unchanged since times immemorial. I haven’t seen a change in the period end closing process (WIP, Variance and Settlement) in the last decade.

This is the most significant change to this process since a long time.

 

What’s changed:

I am not going to go over all that’s changed in UPA in this post, if you are not familiar with UPA you need to first start with the excellent blog posts by Janet and Sarah on this topic

UPA Overview by Sarah

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/universal-parallel-accounting-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13551060

 

Inventory accounting in UPA by Janet

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/inventory-accounting-with-universal-parallel-accounting-in-sap-s-4hana-2022/ba-p/13529230

 

Event-based production accounting in SAP S/4HANA is a significant change from the traditional period-based approach, with cost postings now linked to business events rather than period-end processes. This provides real-time insights and simplifies period-end closing.

Here are the key changes:

  • Real-time insights: Cost information is up to date, allowing comparison of planned versus actual costs and variance splits.
  • Simplified period-end: Traditional period-end activities are removed.
  • Parallel accounting: Enables valuation for different accounting standards, using multiple ledgers and currencies.
  • Posting: Journal entries are created with each business transaction, replacing legacy tables.
  • WIP and Variance Calculation: Calculated immediately upon business events, rather than at period-end.
  • Reporting: New Fiori apps offer better cost and variance reviews.
  • Costing: Event-based costing sheets are necessary.
  • Results analysis key: The RSEBW key is needed for event-based posting.
  • Overhead: Material and production overheads are charged immediately.
  • Transition: Can be implemented by switching order type assignments, however period end steps may still be needed temporarily for both event-based and period-based processes.
  • Settlement: Event-based uses a posting rule, instead of the traditional settlement rule.

The scope item for event-based posting is 3F0, while the traditional approach uses BEI. Event-based is the default in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and is required for universal parallel accounting.

 

 

What’s Changed in Configuration/ Setup?

To configure event-based production accounting, several key settings need to be established within SAP S/4HANA, primarily focusing on the 3F0 scope item. These configurations ensure that costs are posted correctly based on business events rather than at period end.

 

The new IMG Node for EBPA

 

Here are the essential configuration steps,

  • Event-Based Processing Keys: Verify that the event-based processing keys, RSEBW for manufacturing orders and PCCEB for product cost collectors (or ORDMS and PCCMS for manual settlement), are available within the Event-Based WIP and Variance Posting configuration activity. These keys dictate the WIP calculation method, variance split, and valid posting receivers.

Most of the variance and WIP calculation settings are now event based and moved to one single configuration node.

  • WIP Source Accounts: Assign WIP source accounts to specify which costs should be posted and capitalized as WIP and define event-based WIP account determination rules to specify the G/L accounts to which WIP or unrealized costs are to be posted. All WIP source accounts from the Define Assignments configuration activity must be included here.
  • Event-Based Costing Sheets: Create or use a pre-delivered event-based costing sheet in the Define Costing Sheets configuration activity. Ensure the "Event-Based" checkbox is selected for the costing sheet.

 

  • Valuation Variants: Assign the event-based costing sheet to the relevant valuation variants per plant in the Define Valuation Variants per Plant configuration activity. This is crucial for overhead calculations.
    • The valuation variant must have the event-based costing sheet assigned. You can’t assign a non-event-based costing sheet.
  • Order-Type-Dependent Parameters: Assign the event-based processing key (RSEBW or ORDMS) and a planned costing variant to the relevant order types (e.g. YBM1) in the Define Order-Type-Dependent Parameters for Production Orders and Define Order-Type-Dependent Parameters for Process Orders configuration activities. For product cost collectors, assign the event-based processing key PCCEB or PCCMS in Define Cost-Accounting-Relevant Default Values for Order Types and Plants. If using RSEBW or PCCEB, the assigned planned costing variant must have an event-based costing sheet assigned to it.

            

 

  • Account Determination: Ensure that the accounts for posting variances are set up correctly under the Sourcing and Procurement area, within the Automatic Account Determination configuration activity. This includes accounts for price differences (PRD), inventory posting (BSX) and offsetting accounts for inventory posting (GBB).
  • Results Analysis Key: When creating order master data, the RA Key (results analysis key) field must be set to RSEBW for event-based production cost posting. The Event-Based Posting checkmark will then be derived automatically.
  • Settlement: With the event-based approach, settlement profiles are no longer needed. The settlement type is determined by the event-based processing key. If using RSEBW or PCCEB, settlement is triggered by business events, while ORDMS or PCCMS require manual settlement.

By completing these configurations, the system is set up to automatically post WIP, overhead, and variance in real-time, based on business events rather than waiting for period-end activities.

Event based production Postings during the month

The below table shows how WIP and variances are calculated at various stages of using Event based production accounting.

 

Original Order Status

Action to the Order

New Order Status

Event-Based Posting Results

REL (Released) or PDLV (Partially delivered)

Goods issue or activity confirmation

REL (Released) or PDLV (Partially delivered)

WIP increases.

REL (Released)

Partial goods receipt

PDLV (Partially delivered)

WIP decreases.

REL (Released) or PDLV (Partially delivered)

Final goods receipt

DLV (Delivered)

WIP is cleared and variance is settled.

Manually set Complete Technically

TECO (Technically completed)

Manually select Delivery Completed

DLV (Delivered)

TECO (Technically completed) or DLV (Delivered)

Goods issue or activity confirmation

TECO (Technically completed) or DLV (Delivered)

Variance is recalculated and resettled.

TECO (Technically completed)

Manually revoke technical completion

REL (Released)

Variance is reversed and WIP is recalculated and reposted.

DLV (Delivered)

Cancel final goods receipt

PDLV (Partially delivered)

Manually unselect Delivery Completed

 

Now, I am going to create a production order and post a goods issue to show the calculation during the month. We are going to track the progress using the apps below

  • Display Line Items - Production Accounting (F7288)
  • Work in Process - Event-Based (F3498)
  • Analyze Production Costs - Event-Based (F4059)
  • Product Cost Collector Details - Event-Based (F6965)
  • Analyze Costs by Work Center/Operation (F3331)
  • Production Cost Overview - Event-Based (F6248)
  • Display Posting Rules - Event-Based (F5228)

Step 1: Create a new Production order

Event based production costs at order creation using the app “Analyze Production costs, Event Based”

At creation, no actual costs posted yet

Step 2: Post Goods receipt and Confirmation for the Production order with minor variances in quantity

WIP Is calculated and posted right at the point of goods Issue

FI Document for WIP Posting

 

The above screenshot clearly demonstrates advantages UPA and EBPA

  • Its shown that WIP gets posted during the month in Realtime across all ledgers
  • Every ledger has a separate WIP calculation as the cost estimates by ledger are different. i.e. 0L is Legal, 2L is IFRS, 4G is group valuation.
  • WIP is calculated differently for each ledger and valuation.
  • The value is different per each ledger because each ledger has a different costing variant, valuation variant, Overhead costing sheet, Different activity prices, and material prices.
  • You can also see that 2L ledger is having a different fiscal year variant (V3) instead of K4. i.e for the first time ever in SAP we can have more than one fiscal year variant in controlling. This is only possible with UPA.
  • While 0L and 4G post in 2025, 2L posts in 11/2024 (V3 fiscal year variant)

 

How to manage errors during month end

Since we are moving from a periodic posting of WIP and variances to real-time, we need to think closely about error handling.  The below apps allow for monitoring and handling errors during the month.

 

  • Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Overhead Calculation (F5763)
  • Order Exception Monitor - Event-Based (F5133)
  • Manage Product Costing Errors (F5132)
  • Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing (F3669)
  • Schedule Product Costing Jobs (F3683)
    • Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Product Costing (Job Template)
    • Postprocess Event-Based Postings - Overhead Calculation
    • WIP Quantity Calculation for Actual Costing: Event-Based Order and PCC (only if WIP revaluation is needed in Actual Costing (33Q))

 

Errors can be monitored using the above applications.

Conclusion

Event-Based Production Accounting (EBPA) in SAP S/4HANA represents a significant paradigm shift in how companies manage their production accounting processes. This new approach offers several key advantages:

  1. Real-time insights: By posting costs as they occur, EBPA provides up-to-date financial information, enabling more timely decision-making.
  2. Simplified period-end closing: The elimination of traditional period-end activities streamlines the closing process, potentially reducing workload and improving efficiency.
  3. Enhanced parallel accounting: EBPA supports multiple ledgers and currencies, facilitating compliance with various accounting standards simultaneously.
  4. Improved cost visibility: New Fiori apps offer better tools for reviewing costs and variances, enhancing cost control and analysis capabilities.
  5. Automated overhead allocation: Material and production overheads are charged immediately, providing a more accurate picture of costs throughout the production process.

However, implementing EBPA requires careful configuration and a shift in mindset from periodic to event-driven accounting. Companies must ensure proper setup of event-based processing keys, WIP source accounts, costing sheets, and valuation variants. Additionally, they need to establish robust error handling processes to manage issues that may arise during real-time postings.

 

While the transition to EBPA may present initial challenges, the long-term benefits of more accurate, timely, and flexible production accounting make it a valuable evolution for organizations using SAP S/4HANA. As companies continue to adapt to this new approach, they are likely to discover further opportunities for optimization and improved financial management in their manufacturing processes.

 

Q&A


Feel free to ask any questions or share your experience in the comments section below. I will try to answer questions as soon as I can. Alternatively, you can reach out to me on my LinkedIn page below. Follow me there for further updates,

https://ca.linkedin.com/in/naveen-pidikiti

entry.title: Parcel, LTL, FTL Processing with Advanced Shipping & Receiving in SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
This blog post explains how companies (like OEMs) can process their outbound freight from their distribution centers where the Warehouse and Transportation functions are well integrated allowing a possibility to automate the Transportation Planning and Warehouse execution function.

During Transportation Planning, focus is high on standard Vs. express shipping conditions agreed with the customer and in turn deciding when and what to be shipped as Parcel or LTL/FTL. As we know, each of this impacts the delivery SLAs and Costs incurred.

With Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) functionality warehousing and transportation, functions together seamlessly, to achieve key benefits like:

  • Facilitate and prioritize the Pick, Pack, Stage and Load operations for Express shipments
  • Avoid a situation where pallets prematurely occupy and block staging area space waiting for the transport to arrive.

What is Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) 

Let us understand this process from the perspective of an OEM selling Machines, Tools, Equipments and Spare parts.

Dealers of this OEM places orders of different kinds, some are to replenish inventory which means large number of order lines for equipments, urgent orders with very few line items for spares and some orders which are mix of equipments and spares. 

OEM wants to keep the transportation cost as minimum as possible and normally negotiates rates with Parcel Companies and LTL/FTL Carriers. Thing to note here is LTL/FTL is palletized cargo where as Parcels are just loose cartons.

Shipping conditions are enforced by the Dealers while placing the order, normally Standard Shipping or Express Shipping (in case of urgency).

Parcel has its own definition on "Shipment Size and Weight threshold" as published by different service providers like FedEx, UPS, DPD, Postal companies etc. Which also means, not every Express Shipment is a Parcel Shipment.

OEM decides the best way to ship the order lines making sure that delivery is on time as also the cost is kept minimum.

 

Shipping Condition

Qualifies for Parcel 

Shipping Possibility 1Shipping Possibility 2ASR-Type
ExpressYesParcel Warehouse Driven
ExpressNoLTLFTLTransport Driven
StandardYesLTL ParcelTransport Driven
StandardNoLTLFTLTransport Driven

Technical Implementation:

  1. As demonstrated in the "process flow" below, Sales Order information is fed into the SAP TM Freight Unit Builder (FUB) to decide what can be shipped "Parcel" and what can go LTL/FTL. Invoking a BRF+ decision table Parcel related "Shipment Size and Weight Threshold" can be checked. (FUB) spits out Parcel and Non-Parcel Freight Units
  2. Parcel Carrier determination for the Parcel Freight Units is done based on the available Parcel Rate Contracts stored in the SAP TM.
  3. Express Parcels need immediate warehouse execution, such Freight Units are so identified as Warehouse Driven ASR scenario. Functionality of Delivery initiation for Unplanned Freight Units is used to initiate the Warehouse activitiesParcel & Non Parcel Process Flow
  4. Using automated consolidation jobs Non-Express Parcels and Non-Parcel Freight Units can be consolidated into LTL/FTL shipments and follow the Transport Driven ASR process. This avoids premature warehouse execution, which could block the warehouse capacity.
  5. Non-Express Parcel Freight Units which could not be shipped under consolidated LTL load can still be pushed as Parcel Warehouse driven process.
  6. Parcel Shipping Labels can be printed from the Packing Station in the Warehouse

Such end-to-end automated process running daily has a potential to increase the efficiency of the Distribution Center.

entry.title: Warum Change Management bei einer SAP Cloud Migration wichtig ist - ein Blick in die Praxis
entry.content:
In der aktuellen Folge des EducationNewsCast Podcast diskutierte ich mit Philipp Blaesing von Siemens Healthineers und Anja Scheurer von SAP die entscheidende Rolle des Change Managements bei SAP Cloud Migrationen, insbesondere im Rahmen des RISE-Programms. Hier könnt ihr reinhören auf 🎧 SAP Podcasts - Apple - Spotify. Anbei eine Zusammenfassung als Blog. 

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers: Eine globale IT-Initiative

Das RISE-Projekt bei Siemens Healthineers ist eine der größten IT-Initiativen des Unternehmens. Es umfasst die Migration von über 20 SAP-Systemen und angrenzenden Applikationen in die SAP Private Cloud.

Philipp Blaesing betont: "Innovation hat bei uns einen hohen Stellenwert bei Siemens Healthineers. Und da in Zukunft alle Innovationen in der RISE Cloud entwickelt werden, hat natürlich auch die Migration bei uns eine sehr hohe Visibility und Wichtigkeit."

Technische Migration vs. Geschäftsprozess-Transformation: Ein zweistufiger Ansatz

Ein interessanter Aspekt des Projekts ist die Trennung von technischer Migration und Geschäftsprozess-Transformation. Blaesing erklärt: "Wir trennen die technische Migration von den Business-Transformationsprojekten. [...] Erst ein stabiles Fundament gießen, wenn man mal so eine Metapher von einem Hausbau sieht und dann kann man die Räume draufbauen, wie man sie will."

Obwohl es sich primär um ein technisches Projekt handelt, unterstreicht Anja Scheurer die Bedeutung des Change Managements:

"Auch wenn RISE-Projekte auf den ersten Blick eben nur als ein technisches Projekt scheinen, betreffen technologische Veränderungen, technologische Transformationen immer die Menschen, die sie dann schlussendlich auch natürlich anwenden müssen."

Die Rolle des Change Managements in technischen Migrationsprojekten

Die Zusammenarbeit zwischen Projekt- und Change Management ist eng verzahnt.
